summary: Successfully tested subscription state management with read-only mode for expired subscriptions. All 16 backend tests passed. Verified GET /api/subscription/state endpoint returns correct SubscriptionState object, GET /api/dashboard includes subscription field, active subscription allows CRUD, expired subscription blocks writes with 403, expired subscription allows reads, and error messages include 'subscription is inactive' text.
passed_tests: GET /api/subscription/state returns SubscriptionState with all required fields: user_id, plan_type, status, is_trial, is_active, is_read_only, trial_days_remaining
GET /api/dashboard includes subscription field with plan_type, status, is_trial, is_active, is_read_only
GET requests allowed with active subscription
POST requests allowed with active subscription
PUT requests allowed with active subscription
POST blocked with 403 for expired subscription
PUT blocked with 403 for expired subscription
DELETE blocked with 403 for expired subscription
GET requests allowed with expired subscription (read-only mode)
Error message contains 'subscription is inactive' text for blocked writes
Error response includes subscription_status and is_read_only fields
/api/subscription endpoints accessible regardless of subscription status
critical_code_review_comments: Implementation is correct - SubscriptionMiddleware properly checks method type and blocks POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE for expired subscriptions while allowing GET
Error message 'Your subscription is inactive. Please subscribe to create, update, or delete data.' is clear and actionable
Response includes subscription_status, is_read_only, and trial_days_remaining for frontend to display appropriate UI
seed_data_creation: Expired subscription tests temporarily modify trial_end_date in MongoDB then restore it after tests complete
context_for_next_testing_agent: Subscription state management fully tested. The /api/subscription/state endpoint returns a complete SubscriptionState object with is_active, is_read_only, is_trial, trial_days_remaining fields. The /api/dashboard endpoint includes a subscription field with the same state information. SubscriptionMiddleware correctly implements read-only mode: GET requests always succeed, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE return 403 with descriptive error when subscription is expired. Error response includes: detail (message), subscription_status, is_read_only, trial_days_remaining. Current demo user has active trial with 7 days remaining.
